turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Talking to a Bank About a Card Problem

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hello… I have some problem with my bank card. It didn’t work yesterday.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, I can help you with that. Let’s take a look together. Can you tell me what happened when you tried to use the card?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I was in supermarket. I try to pay, but machine say declined. I try two times.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, so the card was declined at the supermarket. Thank you for explaining. Did this happen just once, or has it happened at other places as well?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Just one place. After that, I didn’t try again. I was little worried.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s understandable. There are a few possible reasons why a card can be declined, so we’ll check them one by one. First, do you have the card with you right now?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, I bring it.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great. And do you also have some identification with you, such as a passport or a student card?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Identification… Sorry, what do you mean?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem. Identification means a document that shows who you are, for example a passport or a student ID.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah, okay. I have passport with me.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. Thank you. Now, one common reason for a declined card is that the daily spending limit has been reached. Do you know what your daily limit is?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Daily limit… I hear before, but I don’t really know. Is it like money per day?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s right. It’s the maximum amount of money you can spend using your card in one day.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay… I think I don’t change that before.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s fine. Another possible reason is the account balance. Let me check your account for you now. Okay, I can see your balance here. You currently have one hundred and twenty dollars in your account.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sorry… one hundred twenty? Or two hundred twenty?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: One hundred and twenty — one two zero — dollars.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Oh, okay. One hundred twenty. That makes sense.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes. The purchase you tried to make was one hundred and fifty dollars, so the card was declined because there wasn’t enough money in the account.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah… I see. I didn’t check before. That’s my mistake.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay. It happens quite often. Would you like me to explain how you can check your balance and spending limit using the bank app?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. I have app, but I don’t use it much. I’m not very good with it.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem at all. I’ll explain it slowly. First, you open the app and log in. Then you go to the section called “Accounts”, where you can see your balance. After that, you can go to “Card settings” to see or change your daily limit.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Card settings… Okay. I think I saw that before.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s the one. You can increase the limit if you need to, but it’s a good idea to keep it at a safe amount.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. If I put more money, then card will work, right?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly. Once you add more money to the account, the card should work normally again.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Alright. I will transfer money today.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds good. Is there anything else you’d like help with while you’re here today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Um… one more thing. If card declined again, what should I do?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: If that happens, you can check your balance and limit in the app first. If everything looks fine and it still doesn’t work, you can contact us or come back to the branch, and we’ll help you.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. I understand now. Thank you for explaining.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome. I’m glad I could help.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you.
